calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jun-10-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jennifer Beerline for Robert & Donna Saunders.

The Court expressed concern that statutory fees were taken without a court order, that it understands that the fees are earned, that the court has a major issue with the planned distribution and that the probate examiner put together an alternative plan. Ms. Beerline stated that there had been miscommunication between her firm and the client regarding fees, that initially he was not going to take fees and when he decided that he would he was not aware that he needed court authorization and that the invoice sent out by her firm was a clerical error. Ms. Beerline further stated that they do not approve the probate examiner's distribution plan, that they want the court to approve the distribution as originally requested, explained the mechanics of the plan and that there are no objections. The Court asked why the would not recalculate the distribution amounts. Ms. Beerline explained that while it makes sense, Donna Saunders and her tax advisors objected, that the built in capital gains would have a negative tax consequence on her, that the current plan makes the capital gain 50/50 and confirmed that the portion of the estate that each would end up with would be the same. The Accounting Petition was taken under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)
